description: A Windows 11 workspace + focus manager that leans on OS-native facilities and stays off the input critical path.
pre_install: # Stop any running winspace before Scoop swaps files on disk (ADR-0019): a
# running winspace.exe locks its own image, so scoop update/uninstall fails
# while it runs. Bounded to THIS app's install root, and it leaves the
# autostart Logon task UNTOUCHED, so a failed update never disables autostart
# (post_install re-stamps it). Graceful control-channel quit is reserved for
# 'winspace uninstall' (pre_uninstall), where a real removal warrants it; a
# background update force-stops, and the OS reclaims the hotkeys/hooks on exit.
post_install: # Re-stamp the autostart Logon task to this new versioned location if the
# user's start_at_login is on (the moved-binary self-heal, ADR-0019) WITHOUT
# launching the WM. 'winspace install' respects the config flag as the single
# source of truth and never enables autostart on its own. Stop-and-leave-stopped:
# no restart on update; autostart (if enabled) brings winspace back next logon.
pre_uninstall: # Remove the \winspace\<user> Logon task and gracefully stop the running
# instance so the exe unlocks for deletion (ADR-0019). 'winspace uninstall' is
# hybrid: it messages a live Primary (remove-autostart + graceful quit,
# with a force-kill timeout fallback) or removes the task directly when none is
# running. An absent task counts as success, so this is a clean no-op on a
# half-installed app. Running the exe does not need write access, so invoking
# it while the Primary still holds the image lock is fine.
notes: winspace is installed but NOT started, and autostart is left as your config dictates.
Run 'winspace' to start the window manager in the background.
Config lives at %USERPROFILE%\.config\winspace\winspace.conf (self-seeded on first run) and survives update/uninstall.
To start winspace at logon, set 'start_at_login = true' in your config, then run 'winspace install'.
